environment.dotnetSdk: 10.0.301 (pinned by global.json, rollForward latestPatch); test runtime .NET 10.0.10
environment.supplementaryLocalChecks: macOS arm64, .NET SDK 10.0.301, EnableWindowsTargeting=true Release build (0 warnings, 0 errors) and a scratch net10.0 xunit run of the M4 sources (143/143 passed); local execution of the WPF test assembly is not possible because it requires the Windows Desktop runtime

validatedActions.action: Release build and static analysis on Windows

validatedActions.details: M4 coverage (141 new tests): destination rejection matrix (UNC, network and non-fixed drive types, relative paths, Windows system and application-install directories, reparse points in the path chain); OneDriveData/_TransferReport layout creation and foreign-content detection; SQLite destination binding (bind new, same-operator and other-authorized-operator resume, foreign tenant/drive/employee rejection, non-empty destination without valid state, unsupported future schema version, corrupt database rejected and never reset with original bytes preserved); exclusive lock contention and release; session orchestration with lock-before-state-write and lock release on failed binding; PathMappingVersion=1 vectors (NFC normalization, _xHHHH_ encoding, reserved device names, _empty_ token, ordinal case-insensitive and file-versus-folder collisions, deterministic 10/20/full SHA-256 suffix expansion, 200 UTF-16 code-unit truncation retaining suffix and extension without splitting surrogates, determinism across runs, parent scoping); canonical containment, traversal and rooted-path rejection, stable path-length failure beyond 32767 UTF-16 code units, reparse-point and multi-hard-link overwrite refusal; capacity service with the fixed 5 GiB reserve and strictly-greater boundary; NTFS broad-exposure ACL evaluation logic; user-facing error shape and reference-code uniqueness; DI registration of all M4 services.
78 files scanned for 15 prohibited patterns. No matches.

ciEvidence.earlierFailedRunNote: Commit 83c2f6b failed 2 Windows-only tests (SQLite pooled connection kept a file lock on the corrupt-database test; PathTooLongException was mapped to the containment error instead of the stable path-length error). Fixed in 2861f85; the failed run is retained as history and is not used as evidence.

implemented: local fixed-drive destination validation and OneDriveData/_TransferReport layout
SQLite destination binding to tenant ID, source drive ID, and employee Entra object ID with operator audit only (D-018, D-032)
OS-backed exclusive destination locking (FileShare.None lock file)
authorized-resume validation seam: same tenant/employee/drive/state checks required; operator identity is audit data
deterministic PathMappingVersion=1 with collision-registry seam for M5 persistence
canonical containment, traversal, reparse-point, hard-link, and path-length write guards (D-011)
capacity service: known remaining bytes plus fixed 5 GiB reserve, strictly-greater boundary, per-file recheck
NTFS broad-exposure evaluation (Everyone, Authenticated Users, BUILTIN\Users) behind a testable seam
notImplemented: scan, delta inventory, or dry-run summary (M5)
file transfer, downloads, verification, or copy resume (M5)
reports, CSV/JSON outputs, or per-run report directories (M6)

statusConclusion.completionLabel: Source Implementation Complete remains unearned (later milestones pending)

unexecutedChecks: cross-process and cross-session destination locking on Windows Server (production-level acceptance, M7)
real DriveInfo network/removable drive rejection and real NTFS ACL reading (validated through seams in unit tests; Windows Server verification in M7)
GetFileInformationByHandle multi-hard-link detection on Windows runtime (gated Windows-only code path; M7)
scan, copy, resume, verification, run states (M5)
limitations: Drive-type, reparse-point, hard-link, and ACL checks are validated through test seams; live Windows Server filesystem behavior remains an M7 acceptance item.
Destination locking is unit-tested for in-process contention and release; cross-process/cross-session evidence is production-level and remains unexecuted.
The path-mapping collision registry registered in DI is in-memory; SQLite persistence of mappings is the M5 integration point against the same interface.
Local macOS checks were supplementary only; mandatory evidence comes from Windows CI on the exact validated commit.
